TX.note: 2025 GOP-drawn map (5 additional GOP-leaning seats sought). SCOTUS overruled the lower court 6-3 on 2026-04-27; map is in place through at least the 2030 cycle. No live dispute found as of verification.

CA.note: Prop 50 Democratic counter-map, voter-approved Nov 2025 (~5 additional Dem-leaning seats). SCOTUS denied Republicans' appeal, clearing it for the 2026 cycle. No live dispute found as of verification.

MO.note: New congressional map enacted for the 2026 cycle. No live dispute found as of verification.

NC.note: New congressional map enacted for the 2026 cycle. No live dispute found as of verification.

OH.note: Ohio Redistricting Commission approved a new map 2025-10-31 (shifts from 10-5 to a 12-3 GOP-leaning split) after the legislature missed its deadline. In place for 2026; no live dispute found as of verification.

UT.note: Court-ordered new map from prior litigation, in place for 2026. No live dispute found as of verification.

FL.note: New map (signed 2026-05-04) eliminates FL-20 outright - see data/vacancies.json (Cherfilus-McCormick resigned 2026-04-21; no special election committed for a district that won't exist after Jan 2027). No live dispute found blocking the map's 2026 use as of verification.

TN.note: Legislature repealed its own mid-decade-redistricting ban and passed a new map 2026-05-07 (Memphis/Shelby County split across CD 5/8/9). A state-court challenge (NAACP/Democratic lawmakers) was rejected; plaintiffs vowed appeal, and a separate federal 3-judge-panel preliminary-injunction motion is pending, but neither currently blocks the map for 2026.

LA.note: CORRECTED from the strategy doc's 'likely, litigation delayed' framing (stale by the time of this seed): SCOTUS's Louisiana v. Callais ruling (2026-04-29) struck the prior map's second majority-Black district as an unconstitutional racial gerrymander. The legislature passed a REPLACEMENT map on 2026-05-29 (28-10 state Senate vote; keeps only one majority-Black district of six seats). That replacement map is itself now under fresh litigation from multiple sides - conservative plaintiffs re-suing over the remaining Black-opportunity district, and the Legislative Black Caucus weighing its own suit. Not settled either direction - do not treat as final without re-checking.

AL.note: Confirms the strategy doc's account: a 3-judge panel blocked Alabama's 2023 map 2026-05-26 (intentional-discrimination finding); SCOTUS reinstated that 2023 map 2026-06-02, relying on the narrowed VRA standard from Louisiana v. Callais. Gov. Ivey's Aug 11, 2026 special primary (CD 1/2/6/7) and the Nov 3 general both run under the reinstated 2023 map. The underlying Milligan v. Allen challenge continues in the background but does not currently block the map.
